title: betula verrucosa var. hybrida
intro: Betula verrucosa var. hybrida, also known as the hybrid birch, is a variety of the birch tree, characterized by its unique combination of characteristics from the white birch (Betula papyrifera) and the paper birch (Betula verrucosa). The bark and leaves of the hybrid birch have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Betula verrucosa var. hybrida include reducing fever, improving skin conditions, and acting as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of the hybrid birch include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to treat skin irritations and internally to promote overall well-being.
